subject_tmpl: Working Structure Fire — {ADDRESS}, NFIRS #{CASE} (Report Attached)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, for your awareness ahead of the board packet — we had a working structure fire at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} at {TIME}. Incident type 111 (building fire). On arrival {UNITS} found fire showing from the second-floor rear; companies stretched a 1-3/4 line and made an aggressive interior attack with a primary search. Fire was knocked down and declared under control in approximately 22 minutes; no extension to exposures. Two adult occupants self-evacuated; no civilian or firefighter injuries. Preliminary loss estimate is {AMOUNT} (structure and contents). Cause is under investigation by our fire marshal — see the separate origin-and-cause note to follow. Full NFIRS report {CASE} is attached for the record.
reply_tmpl: Thank you, Chief. Glad everyone got out. Please keep me posted on the cause finding and let me know if the family needs Red Cross coordination.

subject_tmpl: Vehicle Fire — Mannheim Rd at {CROSS}, Incident Report for File (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, documenting a vehicle fire for the file. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a fully involved passenger vehicle on Mannheim Rd at {CROSS}. NFIRS type 131 (passenger vehicle fire). Crews protected an adjacent vehicle and the roadway, knocked the fire with a single 1-3/4 line and dry chem, and overhauled. The driver had exited safely before our arrival — no injuries. Vehicle is a total loss; estimated loss {AMOUNT}. No fuel runoff reached the storm inlet. Roadway was reopened in about 30 minutes with PD traffic control. Report {CASE} is filed; cause appears mechanical/electrical, not suspicious.

subject_tmpl: FYI: Cooking Fire Confined to Stovetop — {ADDRESS}, No Extension (Report Filed)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, brief FYI on a contained cooking fire so you're not surprised if a resident mentions it. {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to {ADDRESS} for a stovetop/grease fire. NFIRS type 113 (cooking fire confined to container). The occupant had already smothered most of it with a lid; crews confirmed no extension into the cabinets or wall, checked the void spaces with the TIC, and ventilated light smoke. No injuries, no transport. Minimal damage — estimated loss {AMOUNT}, mostly the range and cabinet face. We left a working smoke-alarm reminder and a stovetop-safety handout. Report {CASE} closed.
reply_tmpl: Good — thanks for the heads-up. Appreciate the crew leaving the safety materials.

subject_tmpl: Commercial Structure Fire — {BIZ}, {ADDRESS} (NFIRS #{CASE}, MABAS Box Pulled)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, reporting a significant commercial fire at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS}, on {DATE} at {TIME}. NFIRS type 111. First-arriving company reported heavy fire through the roof of the one-story commercial structure and pulled a MABAS Box Alarm — three additional engines and a tower from neighboring departments responded. We transitioned to a defensive operation given roof conditions; master streams held the fire to the original occupancy with partial protection of the attached tenant space. No injuries to occupants or firefighters. Loss is substantial, preliminarily {AMOUNT}. The fire marshal is conducting origin-and-cause; a separate MABAS summary and investigation note will follow. NFIRS report {CASE} attached.
reply_tmpl: Thanks, Chief — please loop in the village manager and our PIO before any media calls come in. I'd like the MABAS summary for the next board meeting.

subject_tmpl: Natural Gas Leak — Lines Bagged, Block Evacuated at {ADDRESS} (Haz Condition Report)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, hazardous-condition report for the record. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to {ADDRESS} for a strong odor of natural gas. NFIRS type 412 (gas leak — natural gas or LPG). Metering showed elevated LEL readings inside the residence and at the meter set. Crews shut the gas at the curb, ventilated, and evacuated three homes as a precaution until Nicor arrived to secure the service line — a contractor had struck the line during landscaping. No fire, no explosion, no injuries. Residents returned within the hour once readings cleared. No dollar loss. Report {CASE} filed; we've flagged the dig-without-locate issue for follow-up.
reply_tmpl: Good work keeping everyone safe. Please make sure JULIE/locate follow-up gets documented in case there's a liability question.

subject_tmpl: Odor of Gas Investigation — No Leak Found, {ADDRESS} (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, documenting a gas-odor investigation that turned out negative. {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} checked {ADDRESS} after a resident reported a gas smell. We metered the interior, basement, appliances, and meter set — all readings zero, no LEL. NFIRS type 671 (hazmat investigation, no hazmat found) with a note. The odor was traced to a recently serviced sewer ejector, not gas. We advised the homeowner and cleared. No evacuation, no injuries, no loss. Report {CASE} closed. Flagging only because the resident was quite concerned and may call your office for reassurance.

subject_tmpl: CO Alarm — Elevated Readings, Family Relocated from {ADDRESS} (Report Filed)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, CO incident for your awareness. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a carbon-monoxide alarm at {ADDRESS}. NFIRS type 424 (CO incident). Our meters confirmed elevated CO — readings in the 90–110 ppm range on the first floor, higher near the furnace. A cracked heat exchanger was the source. We ventilated to zero, shut down the furnace, and red-tagged it. One occupant, age {AGE}, reported a headache and was evaluated by our medics; the family of four relocated to a relative's home for the night. No transports required. Nicor and the homeowner's HVAC contractor notified. Report {CASE} filed.
reply_tmpl: Thank you. Please confirm the family had somewhere warm to go — if not, let's get social services involved tonight.

subject_tmpl: CO Detector Activation, No CO Present — {ADDRESS} (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, routine CO-alarm report. {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a CO detector sounding at {ADDRESS}. NFIRS type 736 (CO detector activation due to malfunction). On arrival our metering found zero CO throughout the residence. The detector was over ten years old and end-of-life. We advised the resident to replace the unit and left a free combination smoke/CO alarm from our public-education stock. No injuries, no relocation, no loss. Report {CASE} closed. Just keeping a paper trail since CO calls are tracked separately in our monthly stats.

subject_tmpl: Hazmat / Fuel Spill at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS} — Incident Report for Your Review (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, hazmat incident report for your review. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} for a diesel fuel spill from a ruptured saddle tank on a delivery truck. NFIRS type 411 (combustible/flammable spill). Estimated 40 gallons released onto the lot; crews diked and contained the product with absorbent and prevented entry into the storm drain. We requested the regional hazmat team for cleanup oversight and notified IEPA per spill-reporting requirements. No fire, no injuries. Cleanup contractor remediated the lot. No structural loss; cleanup costs are the responsibility of the trucking company. Report {CASE} attached for the file.
reply_tmpl: Thanks, Chief. Confirm IEPA notification is documented and let me know if there's any cost exposure to the village.

subject_tmpl: MVA with Extrication — {STREET} at {CROSS}, EMS/Rescue Report (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, EMS/rescue report on a serious crash. {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a two-vehicle collision at {STREET} and {CROSS}. NFIRS type 322 (vehicle accident with injuries) plus extrication. One driver, age {AGE}, was entrapped; crews stabilized the vehicle and used hydraulic tools to perform a door-and-roof displacement, achieving patient access and removal in approximately 12 minutes. Two patients were transported to Loyola — one as a trauma activation. PD handled the crash investigation and roadway closure. No fire. No firefighter injuries. Report {CASE} filed. The rapid extrication reflected well on the crew's training.
reply_tmpl: Please pass my thanks to the crew. Glad the extrication went smoothly — let me know the patients' status if appropriate.

subject_tmpl: Cardiac Arrest Save — Crew Commendation Request (Incident {CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing to recognize an excellent outcome and request a crew commendation. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to {ADDRESS} for a witnessed cardiac arrest, patient age {AGE}. NFIRS type 321 (EMS call). Bystander CPR was in progress; our medics took over with high-performance CPR, delivered defibrillation, and achieved return of spontaneous circulation on scene before transport to Loyola. The hospital confirmed the patient was discharged neurologically intact — a true save. I'd like to recommend the responding crew for recognition at an upcoming board meeting. Incident report {CASE} attached. This is the kind of result our cardiac protocol and CPR training are built for.
reply_tmpl: Wonderful news. Absolutely — send me the crew members' names and I'll have Tina prepare recognition for the next board meeting.

subject_tmpl: Lift Assist at Senior Residence — {ADDRESS}, Service Call (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, brief service-call note. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to {ADDRESS} for a public assist — a resident, age {AGE}, had fallen and could not get up but was uninjured and declined transport. NFIRS type 554 (assist invalid). Crew assisted the resident safely back to a chair, checked vitals, and confirmed no injury. This is the resident's third lift assist this quarter; with their permission we made a referral to our community-risk-reduction program for a home fall-risk assessment and possible grab-bar install. No transport, no loss. Report {CASE} closed.

subject_tmpl: Water Rescue — Swift-Water Response at {STREET} Underpass (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, technical-rescue report. During heavy rainfall on {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to the {STREET} underpass for a vehicle stalled in rising floodwater with one occupant, age {AGE}, trapped inside. NFIRS type 365 (watercraft/water rescue). Our crew, in PFDs and tethered, reached the vehicle and removed the occupant to dry ground; the patient was evaluated and refused transport. We placed high-water barricades and notified Public Works to close the underpass. No injuries to personnel. Vehicle was recovered after the water receded; owner loss roughly {AMOUNT}. Report {CASE} filed. Recommend we revisit signage/automatic closure at this chronic flooding location.
reply_tmpl: Agreed on the underpass — please send me a short memo on closure options and I'll route it to Public Works and the engineer.

subject_tmpl: Automatic Alarm — System Malfunction at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS} (False Call, NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, false-call report for tracking. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to an automatic fire alarm at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS}. NFIRS type 735 (alarm system malfunction). On arrival there was no fire, smoke, or hazard; the panel showed a faulty smoke detector head on the second floor that had gone into trouble. We reset and advised management to have their alarm vendor service the device. No loss. This is the third alarm-system activation at this address this quarter — I've started a repeat-activation file and a courtesy letter to the property manager is warranted; please advise if you'd like that to go out under your signature. Report {CASE} filed.
reply_tmpl: Let's send the courtesy letter from the department first. If we get a fourth, then escalate to a fine letter and copy me.

subject_tmpl: Alarm Activation — Burnt Food at School Building, {ADDRESS} (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, quick note on an unintentional alarm. {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a fire-alarm activation at the school at {ADDRESS}. NFIRS type 733 (smoke detector activation, no fire — unintentional). Source was burnt food in the staff kitchen; light smoke set off a hallway detector. Building was already evacuating per protocol when we arrived; we confirmed no fire, ventilated, reset the panel with staff, and authorized re-entry. No injuries, no loss. Evacuation went smoothly and reflected their recent drill. Report {CASE} closed.

subject_tmpl: Working Alarm Activation — Confirmed Fire at {ADDRESS} (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, documenting an alarm that turned out to be the real thing — a good catch by the system. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a commercial alarm activation at {ADDRESS}. Unlike most automatic alarms, this one was a working fire: crews found smoke and a small fire in an electrical room, type 111. The sprinkler had activated and held the fire in check; we completed extinguishment, secured utilities, and ventilated. No injuries. Loss limited to the electrical room and water damage, estimated {AMOUNT} — far less than it could have been without the alarm and sprinkler. Report {CASE} attached; this is a nice example of working life-safety systems for the board.
reply_tmpl: Good outcome and a good talking point. Please flag this one for me when we discuss the sprinkler-ordinance item.

subject_tmpl: Downed Primary Power Line After Storm — {STREET} (Haz Condition, NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, hazardous-condition report from last night's storm. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a downed primary power line arcing across {STREET}. NFIRS type 444 (power line down). Crews isolated the scene, established a hazard zone, and stood by for ComEd to de-energize and clear the line; we kept the roadway closed with PD until the utility secured it. No fire spread to the parkway trees, no injuries, no structures involved. No dollar loss to the village. Report {CASE} filed. This was one of several storm-related calls overnight — a consolidated 800-series storm summary will follow separately.

subject_tmpl: Severe Storm Response Recap — {DATE} Overnight, 800-Series Incident Summary
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, consolidated storm-response summary for the {DATE} severe weather event. Between {TIME} and end of shift, the department ran 14 storm-related incidents (NFIRS 800-series): wind/tree damage, downed power lines, several flooded-basement pump-outs, and one partial roof failure at {ADDRESS}. We pulled in our reserve engine and held over the off-going shift to keep two ambulances and two engines in service. No serious civilian injuries; one firefighter had a minor strain (reported, no lost time). Aggregate property damage across the responses is preliminarily {AMOUNT}. We coordinated closely with Public Works on barricades and ComEd on line hazards. Individual NFIRS reports are filed; this recap is for your storm briefing and the board.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for the thorough recap, Chief. Please make sure the firefighter's strain is documented for workers' comp and pass my thanks to both shifts.

subject_tmpl: Flash-Flooding Pump-Outs — Weekend Storm Incident Summary (NFIRS 800-Series)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, a quick summary of weekend flooding activity. Following heavy rain on {DATE}, {UNITS} and our reserve crews completed multiple basement pump-out service runs across the {STREET} and {CROSS} area, with the heaviest concentration near {ADDRESS}. NFIRS type 811 (flood assessment/assistance) and related service codes. We prioritized homes with water near electrical panels and gas appliances, securing utilities where needed. No injuries; no structure fires resulted. We advised residents to contact their insurers and avoid contact with flood water around outlets. This is informational for the file and your resident-inquiry awareness — several homeowners may reach out about sandbags and future mitigation. Reports filed individually.
reply_tmpl: Thanks. Please coordinate with Public Works on sandbag availability and send me the list of hardest-hit blocks so we can prioritize stormwater follow-up.

subject_tmpl: Origin & Cause — Garage Fire at {ADDRESS} Ruled Accidental (Electrical)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the origin-and-cause investigation for the garage fire at {ADDRESS} (incident {CASE}, {DATE}) is complete. The fire marshal determined the area of origin was the rear wall near a workbench, and the cause was accidental — an overloaded power strip feeding multiple appliances. No accelerants, no indicators of incendiary activity; the case is classified accidental and closed. No police or OSFM referral is needed. Final loss is confirmed at {AMOUNT}. We're using this incident in an upcoming public-education post about extension-cord and power-strip safety. The full investigation report is attached for your records.
reply_tmpl: Thanks for closing the loop. The power-strip safety post is a good idea — coordinate the timing with our PIO.

subject_tmpl: Suspected Arson — Vacant Structure at {ADDRESS}, Referred to OSFM & Police (Incident {CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, sensitive investigation update — please treat as confidential pending the investigation. The fire at the vacant structure at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} (incident {CASE}) shows indicators consistent with an incendiary cause: multiple unconnected points of origin and the smell of an accelerant noted by crews. Our fire marshal has classified the cause as undetermined-pending and has formally referred the matter to the Office of the State Fire Marshal and to the police department for a joint investigation. No injuries; structure was unoccupied. Loss estimate {AMOUNT}. I wanted you and the village manager aware before any media inquiry, as this is the second vacant-property fire in that area this year. I'll brief you as the investigation develops.
reply_tmpl: Understood — keeping this close. Please set up a briefing call with me, the manager, and PD, and let's discuss securing the other vacant properties in that area.

subject_tmpl: MABAS Box Alarm GIVEN — {UNITS} to Structure Fire in Neighboring Town (Summary)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, mutual-aid summary for the board. On {DATE} at {TIME} we were dispatched on a MABAS box alarm to assist a neighboring department at a working apartment fire. We sent {UNITS} under our mutual-aid agreement. Our companies operated on the fire as part of the box assignment for roughly two hours and returned in service with no injuries and no equipment damage. While our units were committed, our reserve engine covered the village and there were no concurrent emergencies here. There is no cost to us under the reciprocal MABAS agreement. This is the kind of give-and-take that keeps our own box cards staffed when we need help. Aid-given is logged; quarterly MABAS given/received totals will appear in the monthly report.
reply_tmpl: Thanks, Chief. Good to know our coverage held while units were out. Keep the quarterly given/received tally handy for the board.

subject_tmpl: MABAS Box Alarm RECEIVED — Box Pulled for Apartment Fire at {ADDRESS} (Summary)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, mutual-aid summary for an incident in the village. At our working apartment fire at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} (incident {CASE}), the incident commander pulled a MABAS Box Alarm to bring additional resources. Aid received: three engines, one tower ladder, and an ambulance from neighboring departments, plus a change-of-quarters company to cover our station. With the box, we held the fire to the unit of origin and two adjacent units; all occupants were evacuated and accounted for, no serious injuries. Estimated loss {AMOUNT}. Mutual-aid companies were released and returned home within three hours. This summary documents resources committed for the board packet; a full origin-and-cause note will follow.

subject_tmpl: Monthly Fire/EMS Run Statistics — {DATE} (NFIRS Summary for Board Packet)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, here is the monthly NFIRS run-statistics summary for {DATE}. Total incidents: 312. By NFIRS series — fires (100): 11, including 3 working structure fires; rupture/explosion (200): 0; rescue & EMS (300): 214 (our largest category, as usual); hazardous condition (400): 18; service calls (500): 26; good intent (600): 21; false alarm/false call (700): 19; severe weather (800): 3. Average turnout time was 1 minute 12 seconds; average response time 4 minutes 38 seconds, within our benchmark. Mutual aid given on 4 incidents, received on 1. Fire-prevention: 47 inspections completed, 9 violation notices issued. Estimated total fire loss for the month was {AMOUNT}. Full breakdown is attached for the board packet.
reply_tmpl: Thanks, Chief. Numbers look solid. Please have the response-time trend chart ready for the council meeting and flag any address with repeat false alarms.

subject_tmpl: Monthly EMS Call Detail (300-Series) for Mayor's Office — {DATE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, the 300-series EMS detail for {DATE} as you requested. EMS/rescue runs totaled 214 — about 69% of all calls. Breakdown: medical (321) 168, MVA with injury (322) 21, extrications (323) 4, lift/invalid assists (320/554 crossover) 14, other rescue 7. Transports: 152, of which 41 were ALS. Average on-scene time 16 minutes. Most frequent complaint categories were chest pain/cardiac, falls, and difficulty breathing. We had one cardiac save this period (separate commendation memo sent). EMS transport billing reconciliation goes to the finance director separately. Let me know if you'd like this cut differently for the board — by time of day or by district is easy to produce.

subject_tmpl: Open-Burning Complaint — Yard Waste Fire at {ADDRESS}, Ordinance Warning Issued
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, documenting an open-burning complaint. On {DATE} at {TIME}, a neighbor reported an illegal open burn at {ADDRESS}. {UNITS} responded and found the resident, age {AGE}, burning yard waste in a backyard pile — not permitted under our ordinance or IEPA open-burning rules. NFIRS type 561 (unauthorized burning). Crews had the resident extinguish the pile; no extension, no injuries, no loss. We issued a written ordinance warning rather than a citation given it was a first complaint at this address, and left an IEPA-compliant disposal handout. Report filed. If complaints recur at this property, the next step is a citation — I'll keep it on the watch list.
reply_tmpl: A warning is fine for a first offense. Keep me posted if it becomes a repeat — some neighbors have been vocal about smoke on that block.

subject_tmpl: Repeat Illegal Burning — {ADDRESS}, Citation Issued (Burn Complaint Record)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, follow-up on a recurring open-burning problem. {ADDRESS} has now generated its fourth open-burning complaint of the season. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} again responded and confirmed an active, unpermitted burn. Given the prior warnings on file, the resident, age {AGE}, was issued an ordinance citation this time (burn complaint record {CASE}). No fire spread, no injuries. I've documented the complaint history in case the citation is contested at adjudication. Several adjacent neighbors have complained of smoke affecting a household member with respiratory issues, which I've noted. Just keeping your office informed since this address has become a known issue.

subject_tmpl: Dispatched & Cancelled En Route — Good Intent Report #{CASE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, brief good-intent note for the record. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} were dispatched to a reported structure fire at {ADDRESS} but were cancelled en route by police, who confirmed it was a controlled outdoor recreational fire, not a structure fire. NFIRS type 611 (dispatched and cancelled en route). No units arrived on scene; no action required. No injuries, no loss. Logging it because the call counts in our run totals and occasionally prompts a resident question about why apparatus was seen heading their way. Report {CASE} closed.

subject_tmpl: Smoke Scare — Steam Mistaken for Smoke at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS} (Good Intent, NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, a good-intent call worth a quick note. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a report of smoke showing from {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. On arrival, what passers-by saw was steam venting from a rooftop laundry/HVAC unit on a cold morning — no fire, no smoke. NFIRS type 651 (smoke scare, odor of smoke). We confirmed conditions with the TIC, checked the roof unit, and cleared. No injuries, no loss. Caller acted in good faith. Report {CASE} filed. Mentioning it only because a couple of residents called it in and may ask whether there was a fire at that business.

subject_tmpl: Water Problem / Basement Flooding Assist — {ADDRESS}, Service Call (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, service-call note. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to {ADDRESS} for a water problem after a sump-pump failure left several inches of water in the basement approaching the electrical panel and furnace. NFIRS type 520/521 (water problem assist). Crews shut down power to the affected circuits as a precaution, assisted with pumping the worst of the water, and advised the homeowner to contact an electrician and their insurer before restoring power. No fire, no injuries. No loss attributable to the response. Report {CASE} closed. Routine, but documenting since these spike during wet weather.

subject_tmpl: Brush/Grass Fire Along {STREET} Embankment — Contained (NFIRS #{CASE})
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, documenting an outside fire. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to a brush/grass fire on the embankment along {STREET} near the rail right-of-way. NFIRS type 142 (brush/grass fire). Dry conditions allowed it to spread to roughly a quarter-acre before our arrival; crews used booster lines and brooms to knock it down and protect a nearby fence line. No structures threatened, no injuries. Cause appears to be discarded smoking material along the path. No significant dollar loss. Report {CASE} filed. With the current dry stretch I've asked crews to watch that corridor; we may post a fire-danger reminder if conditions persist.
reply_tmpl: Good call on the corridor watch. If you want to post a fire-danger advisory, draft it and send to our PIO and me.

subject_tmpl: Gas Explosion — Residential Garage at {ADDRESS}, No Resulting Fire (NFIRS 251 Filed)
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, reporting a rare overpressure/explosion incident. On {DATE} at {TIME}, {UNITS} responded to {ADDRESS} for an explosion in a detached garage. NFIRS type 251 (explosion, no after-fire). Investigation indicates an accumulation of natural gas from a faulty connection on a garage heater ignited, blowing out the overhead door and a wall section — but, notably, there was no sustained fire afterward. One occupant, age {AGE}, sustained minor injuries and was transported for evaluation. We secured the gas and electric, metered the area clear, and requested Nicor and a structural assessment of the adjacent house. Estimated loss {AMOUNT}. Cause-and-origin and utility follow-up are underway; report {CASE} attached. Flagging given the explosion and injury.
reply_tmpl: Thanks for the fast notice, Chief. Please keep me updated on the injured occupant and confirm the adjacent home is structurally safe before anyone re-occupies.

subject_tmpl: Burn Permit Request for Review — Special Event Bonfire, {BIZ}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, a burn-permit request for your awareness before I act on it. {BIZ} has applied for a one-time recreational/special-event bonfire permit for an event at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} at {TIME}. I've reviewed the site against our ordinance and IEPA rules: the proposed pit meets setback distances from structures and property lines, they'll have a charged garden hose and extinguisher on site, and the burn is clean wood only — no yard waste or refuse. I'm inclined to approve with standard conditions (attended at all times, out by a set hour, weather/wind contingency). No fee dispute. Flagging only because it's a public event; if you have any concern I'll hold. Otherwise I'll issue the permit with conditions.
reply_tmpl: No concerns — go ahead and issue with your standard conditions. Just make sure the on-duty shift knows it's permitted so we don't roll apparatus on a complaint call.
